focus: Iteration 1 — exhaustive Claude/OpenCode/Devin hook inventory and initial portability matrix
noveltyJustification: The live tree adds a fifteenth OpenCode entrypoint beyond the README and the current Devin lifecycle page supplies event-specific stdin fields needed for row-level verdicts.

graphEvents.label: OpenCode registrations are not Devin hooks
Claude import covers only compatible command hooks

label: The filesystem has 15 OpenCode plugin entrypoints while the checked-in README enumerates 14; mk-codex-hooks-watchdog.js is omitted.
The live Claude settings contain 19 command handlers across eight event keys; PreCompact is not a Devin lifecycle event.
Native Claude import can cover compatible command-hook registrations but cannot import OpenCode plugin tools/transforms or translate matcher, environment, async, and compaction semantics.
Reuse the seven runtime-neutral guard cores; adapt the Claude/OpenCode transport wrappers to Devin stdin, matcher, cwd, output, and lifecycle semantics.

direction: Treat native Claude import as a complete replacement for the Devin adapter layer
reason: It has no OpenCode plugin registration surface and no direct PreCompact equivalent; tool names and payload/output contracts also differ.
